examples.title: Basic Class Template - Stack
examples.description: A simple generic stack implementation using class template
examples.explanation: Template parameter T is replaced with actual type at compile time. Each instantiation (int, float, string) generates separate class with type-specific code.

examples.title: Template with Multiple Parameters
examples.description: Class template with type and non-type parameters
examples.explanation: Combines type parameter (T) with non-type parameter (MAXSIZE). Non-type parameter must be compile-time constant.

examples.title: Full Template Specialization
examples.explanation: Full specialization (template<>) provides completely new implementation for specific type. Compiler uses specialized version when exact type matches.

examples.title: Partial Template Specialization
examples.explanation: Partial specialization matches type patterns. When T* is used, pointer version is selected with special handling for pointer types.

examples.title: Member Function Template
examples.description: Template member functions inside regular class
examples.explanation: Member function templates allow flexible type conversion and operations. They're instantiated only when called.
